# Story 22.6: Auto-Generated Review and Follow-Up Tasks

**Status:** draft
**Epic:** 22 — Self-Driving Development Pipeline
**Depends on:** Story 22.5

## User Story

As a user,
I want ThreeDoors to automatically create review and follow-up tasks when workers produce results,
So that PR reviews and CI fixes appear naturally in my door rotation.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Given** a worker has completed and a PR has been created
**When** the polling loop detects the completion
**Then:**

- AC1: New task created: "Review PR #N: <original task text>" with status `todo`
- AC2: If CI fails on the PR (detected via worker status or history), new task created: "Fix CI on PR #N: <failure summary>" with status `todo`
- AC3: Generated tasks appear in normal door rotation — they are standard `Task` objects added to the task pool
- AC4: Generated tasks include the original task ID in their `Context` field for traceability: "Auto-generated from task <original-id>"
- AC5: Deduplication: before creating "Review PR #N", check if a task with that text already exists — skip creation if found
- AC6: Auto-generated tasks have `DevDispatch` pre-set with `PRNumber` for traceability
- AC7: Tasks are persisted to the task file via the existing save mechanism

## Quality Gate

- QG1: `make fmt` passes (gofumpt formatted)
- QG2: `make lint` passes with zero warnings
- QG3: `make test` passes — all tests green
- QG4: Rebased on latest main
- QG5: Scope-checked against this story — no extras
- QG6: Errors handled with context wrapping (`%w`)

## Technical Notes

- Task generation happens in the `WorkerStatusMsg` handler after a queue item transitions to `completed`
- Use the existing `TaskPool.Add()` or equivalent to insert generated tasks
- Generated task IDs follow the existing UUID generation pattern
- CI failure detection: check if the `HistoryEntry` status indicates CI failure — the exact field depends on multiclaude output format (may need to parse summary text)
- Don't over-engineer: start with "Review PR #N" only; CI failure tasks can be refined based on actual multiclaude output
- Task text deduplication uses simple string prefix matching: `strings.HasPrefix(task.Text, "Review PR #N:")`

## Dependencies

- Story 22.5 (Worker status polling and WorkerStatusMsg)

## Tasks

1. Implement `generateFollowUpTasks(item QueueItem, originalTask *Task) []*Task` in dispatch package
2. Add deduplication check against existing tasks in the pool
3. Hook task generation into `WorkerStatusMsg` handler in MainModel
4. Persist generated tasks via existing save mechanism
5. Write unit tests for task generation, deduplication, and context tracing
6. Verify all quality gates pass

## Pre-PR Checklist

- [ ] `make fmt` — no formatting changes needed
- [ ] `make lint` — zero warnings
- [ ] `make test` — all tests pass
- [ ] `go test -race ./...` — no race conditions
- [ ] Deduplication prevents repeated task creation
- [ ] Generated tasks appear in door rotation
- [ ] Context field includes original task ID
